subj: THIS IS A VERY LONG SUBJECT LINE INTENDED TO FORCE WRAPPING IN THE SECNAV LETTER GENERATION SYSTEM TO VERIFY THAT CONTINUATION LINES ALIGN PROPERLY UNDER THE FIRST WORD OF THE SUBJECT TEXT AND NOT UNDER THE LABEL
body: 1. First top-level paragraph. This line is intentionally long enough to wrap onto a continuation line so we can confirm continuation returns to the left margin.
a. First subparagraph. This line is intentionally long enough to wrap onto a continuation line so we can confirm subparagraph continuation returns to the left margin.
b. Second subparagraph required because if a subdivision is used, at least two are required.
(1) First nested paragraph. This line is intentionally long enough to wrap onto a continuation line for nested marker testing.
(2) Second nested paragraph required for paired subdivision validation.
(a) First fourth-level paragraph. This line is intentionally long enough to wrap onto a continuation line for fourth-level marker testing.
(b) Second fourth-level paragraph required for paired subdivision validation.
2. Second top-level paragraph after nested material.
10. Tenth top-level paragraph. This line verifies that marker plus two spaces naturally pushes text farther right and paragraphs do not line up.
